user_query:
I want a weekly automated job that compiles a shortlist of compelling official movie posters for my upcoming-release roundup and updates my newsletter draft. Please implement it so I can run it locally right away and also schedule it to refresh weekly.

Inputs provided in the workspace:
- input/releases.csv
- input/newsletter_template.md

Requirements
1) Selection and filtering
- Read input/releases.csv (fields: title, release_date in ISO YYYY-MM-DD, studio, studio_domain_hint).
- On each run, select films whose release_date is within the next 45 days from the run date. If fewer than 5 films qualify, select the 5 soonest upcoming films instead (never select more than 12 total).
- Save the list you actually selected (with all original fields) to output/selection/selected_films.csv.

2) Internet searches (must use a live public search engine)
- For each selected film, run one or more search engine queries, at minimum: "<title> official poster" and optionally "<title> poster <studio>".
- For each film, capture the top 10 results from the search engine page(s) into a structured artifact with: film_title, query_string, run_timestamp (ISO), result_rank (1-based), result_title, result_url, result_snippet (or brief text if available), and source_domain (derived from the URL).
- Save all results as line-delimited JSON at output/search/search_results.jsonl (one JSON object per result). Do not hardcode any URLs in advance; the results must come from the live queries.

3) Ranking poster candidates
- For each film, score each search result using this transparent, deterministic scoring:
  - +5 if source_domain matches or is a subdomain of studio_domain_hint from the CSV.
  - +3 if the result_title or result_url contains the word "poster" (case-insensitive).
  - +2 if the result_title contains the film title phrase (case-insensitive exact phrase match).
- Ties are broken by lower result_rank.
- For each film, select up to the top 3 candidates by the final score. Save an aggregated table to output/posters/poster_candidates.csv with columns: film_title, release_date, studio, candidate_rank (1–3), score, result_title, result_url, source_domain, rationale (comma-separated flags indicating which scoring criteria were met), query_string, run_timestamp.

4) Film ranking summary
- Rank films by their best candidate score (descending). For each film, include best_score, best_source_domain, and best_result_url. Save to output/posters/films_ranked.csv with columns: film_title, release_date, studio, best_score, best_source_domain, best_result_url.

5) Edit and rewrite the newsletter draft
- Using input/newsletter_template.md, produce output/newsletter_ready.md with these edits:
  - Rewrite the [[INTRO_DRAFT]] block into a single polished paragraph in my critic’s voice that highlights the three most compelling posters based on your ranking (do not claim facts not inferable from the rankings; keep it concise and evocative). Replace the entire block (including markers) with your rewritten paragraph.
  - Replace [[TOP_FILMS_TABLE]] with a bulleted list of the top 5 films by best_score, each line formatted as: Title — Release Date — Studio — Best Poster URL.
  - Replace [[PER_FILM_SHORTLIST]] with per-film subsections for all selected films, each including the film title and up to three ranked poster candidate URLs, each with the source_domain and a brief rationale fragment (e.g., "official domain; title includes 'poster'").
  - Preserve other content as-is; do not add external images or download files.

6) Scheduling and logs
- Provide an executable entry script at output/bin/poster_roundup (you may choose the implementation language) that performs all steps above and writes logs.
- Create a cron-style schedule file at output/schedule/weekly_cron.txt containing a single line that runs the entry script at 09:00 every Monday local time and appends logs to output/logs/weekly.log. Do not attempt to install the cron job; just provide the file with the correct line.
- The entry script must support a --once flag that performs an immediate run and writes:
  - Primary outputs under output/latest/
  - A dated archive copy under output/archive/YYYY-MM-DD/
- Write a summary log to output/logs/last_run.log including: run_timestamp, total_films_in_csv, films_selected, total_queries_issued, total_results_captured, and any errors encountered per film.

General constraints
- Do not include direct URLs in this request; all external URLs must originate from live search engine queries at run time.
- If a search fails for a film, log the error, produce zero candidates for that film, and continue.
- All paths mentioned above must be created and populated by your solution so the run is locally verifiable.

Deliverables to verify
- output/selection/selected_films.csv
- output/search/search_results.jsonl
- output/posters/poster_candidates.csv
- output/posters/films_ranked.csv
- output/newsletter_ready.md
- output/bin/poster_roundup (executable)
- output/schedule/weekly_cron.txt (with the 09:00 Monday cron line referencing the entry script and appending to output/logs/weekly.log)
- output/logs/last_run.log
- The entry script must be runnable with --once to produce output/latest/ and output/archive/YYYY-MM-DD/ All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
